items.q: Which mood follows 'je souhaite que' (I wish that)?
items.why: Students use indicative after expressions of wish. SOUHAITER QUE, VOULOIR QUE, AIMER QUE all trigger subjunctive when there's a different subject. Note: espérer que typically takes indicative - a common irregularity.

items.q: How do you say 'I study maths' in French?
items.why: Students drop the article like English. School subjects in French take the DEFINITE article: 'j'aime le français', 'j'étudie les sciences', 'je déteste l'histoire'. This is the rule for general nouns.

items.q: How do you say 'I do not eat' in French?
items.why: Students forget the verb 'sandwich'. Ne and pas wrap AROUND the verb in present tense - ne BEFORE the verb, pas AFTER. In compound tenses, they wrap around the auxiliary.

items.q: On what date did de Gaulle deliver his radio appeal?
items.why: Correct answer: 18 June 1940. “10 May 1940” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: Jean Moulin. “Petain” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: On what day of August 1944 was Paris liberated?
items.why: The real figure is 25. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
